channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump announces more picks, nominates Kimberly Guilfoyle to serve as ambassador to Greece
item.description: President-elect Trump on Tuesday made several announcements of appointments and nominations as he continues to fill out his incoming administration
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DA suggests unusual idea for halting Trump’s hush money case while upholding his conviction
item.description: Eager to preserve President-elect Donald Trump ’s hush money conviction even as he returns to office, prosecutors are suggesting various ways forward — including one based on how some courts handle criminal cases when defendants die.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Report on attempts to kill Trump urges Secret Service to limit protection of foreign leaders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A congressional task force investigating the attempts to kill Donald Trump during his presidential campaign is recommending changes to the Secret Service, including protecting fewer foreign leaders during the height of election season and considering moving the agency out of the Ho
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI leader says it's 'concerning' how little his agency knows about mysterious drones seen over New Jersey
item.description: A top FBI leader revealed the agency knows concerningly little about the mysterious, SUV-sized drones that have been seen hovering over New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell receives medical attention after falling
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) received medical attention after falling following a Republican lunch meeting Tuesday. A spokesman for McConnell said the GOP leader tripped after the lunch. "He sustained a minor cut to the face and sprained his wrist.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: SCOTUS hears arguments in case that could reshape environmental law
item.description: The Supreme Court heard oral arguments in a case to determine the scope of NEPA's requirement for federal agencies to conduct environmental impact reviews.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As session nears, status of a Minnesota lawmaker facing criminal charges remains a flashpoint
item.description: Sen. Nicole Mitchell has upcoming court hearings in a burglary charge ahead of a trial that would start soon after 2025 Legislature gavels in. She’s resisting calls to step down, and a departure would leave the Senate knotted up.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump pressing DeSantis to name Lara Trump as Rubio's Senate successor: source
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is pressing Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis to name Lara Trump as Sen. Marco Rubio's Senate successor, a source told Fox Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bragg lays out alternatives to dismissing Trump's hush money conviction
item.description: Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg’s (D) office opposes dismissing President-elect Trump’s hush money charges but would accept halting the proceedings while he is in office, according to court documents made public Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kash Patel FBI plans spark concern over agency independence, retribution
item.description: Kash Patel hasn’t offered specifics on his plans for the FBI, but he’s made it clear that sweeping changes are on the table.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump lawyers and aide hit with 10 additional felony charges in Wisconsin over 2020 fake electors
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin prosecutors filed 10 additional felony charges Tuesday against two attorneys and an aide to President-elect Donald Trump who advised Trump in 2020 as part of a plan to submit paperwork falsely claiming that the Republican had won the battleground state that year.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Susan Wild absent from Ethics Committee meeting after Gaetz leaks to press
item.description: Rep. Susan Wild (Pa.), the top Democrat on the House Ethics Committee, was absent from the panel’s meeting last week after being traced as the source of leaks to the press regarding the investigation into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s vow to end birthright citizenship could face legal challenges, GOP skeptics
item.description: President-elect Trump has doubled down on his vow to end birthright citizenship, a task that would likely face legal challenges as well as skepticism from within his own party. For starters, the 14th Amendment grants citizenship to those born in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP to choose chairs for influential committees: Who’s in the running
item.description: House Republican leaders will choose new chairs for a number of influential committees this week, people who will have the power to help deliver on President-elect Trump’s priorities in the GOP trifecta over the next two years.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Greene says she is safe after bomb threat
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.) confirmed she is safe after reports swirled of an email sent to law enforcement containing a bomb threat targeting the lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US says it will support new Syrian leaders who protect women and renounce terrorism
item.description: The Biden administration says the United States will recognize and support a new Syrian government that renounces terrorism, destroys chemical weapons stocks and protects the rights of minorities and women.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pete Hegseth, Tulsi Gabbard on Capitol Hill to get Senate support
item.description: Two of President-elect Donald Trump's most controversial cabinet nominees are on Capitol Hill this week.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court leans toward Utah oil railway plan, but may not make broad environmental ruling
item.description: The Supreme Court seemed to favor a Utah oil railroad expansion in arguments on Tuesday, but appeared more skeptical of putting strict new limits on a key national environmental law.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Here's how the US is countering the Islamic State group during Syria's upheaval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Massive U.S. airstrikes on Islamic State militants in Syria were meant in part as a message to the group and a move to ensure that it doesn't try to take advantage of the chaos following the overthrow of President Bashar Assad's government. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Donald Trump says tariffs will not hurt Americans' pockets
item.description: Trump was unwilling to speculate whether the costs of tariffs would be passed on to the American consumer.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats frustrated with Biden's 'lame duck' end to presidency
item.description: With 42 days until President Joe Biden cedes power, some Democrats are exasperated at how he is spending his final days in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada adds blockchain security to prevent repeat of 'fake electors' mischief
item.description: Nevada has integrated blockchain technology into its election certification process to add even more security to its procedures, the Nevada Secretary of State's Office announced on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republicans pick Mast to lead House Foreign Affairs panel
item.description: House Republicans selected Rep. Brian Mast (R-Fla.) to chair the House Foreign Affairs Committee in the 119th Congress, sources told The Hill, as the conference works to organize its panels for the next two years of Republican-controlled Washington Republicans also selected Rep.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Florida State Rep. Susan Valdes, representing parts of Hillsborough County, switches political parties
item.description: State Rep. Susan Valdes, who represents Florida's 64th District, including parts of Hillsborough County, switched political parties, the Florida House of Representatives announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Manchin, Welch unveil bill placing term limits on Supreme Court
item.description: West Virginia Sen. Joe Manchin (I) and Vermont Sen. Peter Welch (D) have introduced a resolution to impose 18-year term limits on Supreme Court justices, which would require some turnover on the high court every two years.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams on City of Yes plan passing City Council vote
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City Council has approved Mayor Eric Adams' ambitious City of Yes plan. The $5 billion plan will rezone the Big Apple for the first time in six decades. It's estimated to create more than 80,000 new housing units over 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Minnesota U.S. Attorney Andy Luger to step down ahead of Trump transition
item.description: U.S. Attorney Andy Luger is stepping down soon as the top federal law enforcement official in Minnesota. As is custom for political appointees, he’s resigning to make way for a yet-to-be-announced successor chosen by incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: NYC plan would abolish income tax for certain residents: See if you qualify
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new proposal would abolish New York City’s personal income tax for nearly half a million qualifying residents.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Talking Sense: Coming together to solve tough problems
item.description: Mariah Levison and Rob Fersh help people find consensus on how to solve divisive issues, ranging from prison reform to what art should hang in the Minnesota Capitol. Learn how they do it in this MPR News Talking Sense conversation hosted by MPR News correspondent Catharine Richert.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis, Catharine Richert, Maja Beckstrom, and Annie Baxter, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy are bringing Trump’s DOGE to Capitol Hill
item.description: The leaders of the so-called “Department of Government Efficiency” are calling for large-scale layoffs of federal workers and the elimination of some federal agencies during Trump's second term.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski joins mayor’s race
item.description: She joins Minnesota DFL Sen. Omar Fateh and the Rev. DeWayne Davis of Plymouth Congregational Church in challenging Mayor Jacob Frey, who’s running for reelection.
item.dc:creator: Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: Trump criticizes Chronister after sheriff's withdrawal, days after praising him
item.description: President Donald Trump challenged reports of Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister's DEA withdrawal, saying he didn't want the sheriff days his cabinet days after nominating him.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas Mayor Shelley Berkley sworn into office; envisions future of prosperity for the city
item.description: Shelley Berkley was sworn in as the mayor of Las Vegas on Wednesday, promising to serve the city well and work with the community to create an atmosphere of growth and prosperity.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s mother says The New York Times made ‘threats’ by asking her to comment on a story
item.description: A basic tenet of journalism — calling someone for comment on a story — was seen as a threat by defense secretary nominee Pete Hegseth’s mother.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump reportedly considering DeSantis as replacement nominee for defense secretary
item.description: Several reports, including from the Wall Street Journal, have said Governor Ron DeSantis could become President-elect Trump's new defense secretary nominee. The news comes amid a growing number of allegations against Trump's original nominee Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC
